name: Pusher Channels WebSocket API
what_it_does: Provides hosted WebSocket infrastructure for real-time pub/sub messaging across web and mobile clients using a simple channel and event model.
use_cases: Push live agent status updates and streaming output to a web dashboard without polling
Broadcast events from a server-side agent to all connected browser clients subscribed to a shared channel
Implement presence channels to show which users or agent workers are currently active in a session
Trigger client-side UI updates from an agent pipeline when a background job completes
Send private encrypted messages between a backend agent and a specific authenticated client
not_for: Durable message queuing or guaranteed delivery to offline consumers (use SQS or a queue service)
High-frequency machine-to-machine messaging between backend services at scale (use Ably or Kafka)
Video/audio streaming or WebRTC (use LiveKit or Agora)
best_when: You need a simple, well-documented WebSocket layer to push server-side events to browser or mobile clients without managing WebSocket infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need message persistence, history replay, or complex routing logic beyond simple channel subscriptions.

auth.notes: App key and secret used for server-side publishing. Private and presence channels require a server-side auth endpoint that signs channel authorization requests — agents must implement this endpoint. Public channels require no auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: Startup $49/mo, Pro $99/mo, Business $299/mo; scales on connections and message volume
pricing.notes: Daily message limit on free tier can be restrictive for high-frequency agents. Connection limit is per app, not per channel.

performance.rate_limits: 10 messages/sec per channel on free tier; higher on paid. REST publish API: 100 events per batch, 10 channels per event trigger

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP API returns standard status codes with JSON error bodies. SDK errors are generally descriptive. Channel auth failures return 403 with a message but limited diagnostic detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys supported. Duplicate event detection is the agent's responsibility. Pusher does not deduplicate published messages.
known_agent_gotchas: Private and presence channels require a live auth endpoint on the agent's server; if that endpoint is down, all private channel subscriptions will fail
The 100-event batch publish limit applies per API call — agents publishing high-frequency events must handle batching logic themselves
Messages are not persisted; if a client disconnects and reconnects, it will miss events published during the disconnection window
Channel names are case-sensitive and have a 200-character limit; agents generating channel names dynamically must enforce this constraint
Pusher's free tier has a hard daily message cap that resets at midnight UTC — agents must implement fallback behavior when the cap is hit

security_score_components.security_notes: Channel authorization tokens are HMAC-signed server-side. App secret must be protected server-side and never exposed to clients. No fine-grained scope system beyond public/private/presence channel types.
